---
title: "Meeting Minutes"
subtitle: "Meeting 1 · Topic selection"
author: "Team HeeHaa"
date: last-modified
toc: true
---

## Details

| | |
|---|---|
| **Date** | Saturday, 6 June 2026 |
| **Time** | 4:00 PM |
| **Present** | Mark Yee, Serene Cheng, Carina Faith Peh |
| **Agenda** | Topic selection for the Visual Analytics Project |

## 1. Agenda

The team met to choose between the two VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenges as the basis for the group project, and to agree on immediate next steps.

## 2. Discussion

### 2.1 MC2 summary — presented by Serene and Carina

Serene and Carina presented MC2 as a forensic investigation of a single anomalous event, namely a gibberish post that appeared on SaidIT on 17 May 2046 under the identity of John Windward (Head of Customer Support, TenantThread) in a random forum, raising the concern of a malfunctioning or manipulated AI agent. The data is a large event log of around 185,000 timestamped system actions plus an org chart of 75 nodes. The challenge asks how the post was made, what the content means and where it came from, and whether the behaviour could recur, ending in one recommended intervention point. The suggested approach is to establish a normal-post baseline, isolate the anomaly, trace the event chain back through agents, files and task delegations, and then recommend a control, with the chain running across 19 agents over eight days.

### 2.2 MC1 summary — presented by Mark

Mark presented MC1, in which TenantThread runs seven AI agents through a PR crisis while a CivicLoom merger sits under embargo. The Judge, a compliance agent, monitors only two of the six channels, yet embargoed information still reaches the public, so the challenge is to determine whether an agent bypassed the rules or the monitoring broke down, and to prove it from the messages. The data is a nested log of 912 messages across 23 rounds and six channels of differing accountability. The approach sets a baseline of normal behaviour and then flags abnormal channel use, bypass posts (public posts with no monitored trace) and changes in the communication network, with every signal traceable back to the message text and the agent's private reasoning.

### 2.3 Comparison and decision

Mark noted that an MC2 application would likely be more straightforward, being a clean investigative analysis of a known incident with less ambiguity, where the answer is largely a traceable chain of events. He felt MC1 is less straightforward because it touches on debatable areas such as where the baseline between the normal and suspect periods is drawn and what actually counts as a breach. The look-back window and flagging thresholds affect the conclusion, so the application has to let the analyst set and test these rather than fix a single answer. Carina agreed and added that MC2 would likely be simpler to build, since the event log gives an explicit timeline of actions and parties, a normal post has a clear structural signature to flag against, and the work follows a fixed investigative sequence ending in one intervention, with few subjective thresholds. Serene expressed that the team should aim for the more challenging and rewarding project and recommended MC1, and everyone concurred.

## 3. Decision

- **Topic selected: Mini-Challenge 1 (MC1).**

## 4. Action items

| Owner | Task | Due |
|---|---|---|
| Mark | Draft the project proposal | Tue, 9 Jun |
| Serene, Carina | Read Mark's MC1 technical report, then add comments and new insights to the proposal | Before submission |
| Team | Submit the proposal | Wed, 10 Jun |
| Mark | Look into creating a new website for the project | Ongoing |
| Serene, Carina | Work out the best way to collaborate using RStudio | Ongoing |

## 5. Next meeting

- **13 June** — refine and finalise the application modules and split the division of work.

---

Sources:

- [Take-Home Exercise 2: VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenge 2 — Carina Peh](https://isss-608-vaa-carinapeh.vercel.app/Take%20Home%20Exercise%202/Take_Home_Ex02.html)
- [Take-Home Exercise 2 — Serene Cheng](https://isss-608-vaa-serenechengjj.vercel.app/Take-Home_Ex/Take-Home_Ex2/Take-Home_Ex02.html)
- [Take-home Exercise 2: TenantThread Embargo Leak (MC1) — Mark Yee](https://isss-608-vaa-my.vercel.app/TH_EX02.html)
